key_event.1900-1909: Dervish revolt; no central state, strong clan society
key_event.1910-1919: Dervish proto-state consolidates at Taleh amid British coastal retreat
key_event.1920-1929: Dervish collapse and Italian conquest of sultanates
key_event.1930-1939: Fascist centralization and absorption into Italian East Africa
key_event.1940-1949: Founding of Somali Youth League; nationalist mass mobilization
key_event.1950-1959: UN trusteeship builds proto-state; elections mobilize clan-based parties
key_event.1960-1969: Independence democracy then 1969 Barre military coup
key_event.1970-1979: Barre's socialist regime builds despotic state, suppresses clan society
key_event.1980-1989: Barre's clan-based despotism collapses; armed clan insurgencies rise
key_event.1990-1999: State collapse (1991); clan norms and warlords fill vacuum
key_event.2000-2009: Statelessness persists; TFG/ICU/al-Shabaab contest a fragmented order
key_event.2010-2019: Federal Government formed; territory reclaimed from al-Shabaab
